description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a bright yellow fuselage and wings, featuring black cross insignias on the upper and lower wings, reminiscent of early 20th-century military aircraft. The airplane has two sets of straight, parallel wings connected by struts and wires, and a single wooden propeller mounted at the front of a red and green radial engine cowling. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the engine, with a small windshield and visible control panel. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, all in matching yellow with control surfaces outlined. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, all fixed and exposed. The overall design is compact, with a short fuselage and a robust, classic appearance.
description: A bright yellow vintage biplane with black cross insignias, open cockpit, and a wooden propeller on a red and green engine cowling. Features dual wings, fixed landing gear, and a classic early aviation design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wooden propeller, red and green engine cowling, and dual yellow wings with black cross insignias.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the stacked biplane wings, exposed engine details, and fixed landing gear.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail section with single vertical stabilizer, horizontal stabilizers, and open cockpit behind the wings.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the biplane wing structure, tail assembly, and cockpit placement.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked vertically, no winglets, and slight dihedral angle.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with exposed radial cylinders and a wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two fixed main wheels and a fixed tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open single-seat cockpit located just behind the engine cowling with a small windshield.
